directions.reason: Eyes, nose, and muzzle lift vertically.
Nose and pupils shift screen-right while the eye line and muzzle remain raised.
Three-quarter face and nose turn right; the raised muzzle reads up in the labeled loop despite blind-axis ambiguity.
Near-profile nose points right and retains a slight upward pitch despite blind-axis ambiguity.
Nose and visible pupil form a clear screen-right profile.
Profile remains right while the muzzle begins dropping below the eye line; vertical blind-axis ambiguity is accepted.
Lowered muzzle stays right of head center and angles down; vertical blind-axis ambiguity is accepted.
Nose remains subtly right of center while eyes and muzzle point strongly down; horizontal blind ambiguity is accepted.
Eyes, nose, and muzzle point unmistakably down.
Nose and visible eye shift subtly left while the gaze remains strongly lowered; horizontal blind ambiguity is accepted.
Muzzle and visible eye occupy the left quadrant while the head remains lowered; vertical blind-axis ambiguity is accepted.
Near-profile nose points left and retains a slight downward pitch; vertical blind-axis ambiguity is accepted.
Nose and visible pupil form a clear screen-left profile.
Profile remains left while the muzzle and eye line begin lifting; vertical blind-axis ambiguity is accepted.
Three-quarter face stays left of center and the lifted muzzle reads up in the ordered loop despite blind-axis ambiguity.
Near-front face retains a subtle left bias while eyes and muzzle lift; horizontal blind ambiguity is accepted.

review_warnings: Intermediate blind-axis ambiguity is accepted because labeled normal-size review confirms intended quadrants without reversal.
Continuity metric outliers show no conspicuous visible snap, scale pop, or broken registration.
Reported alpha-hole candidates are natural inter-ear or silhouette negative space, with no visible interior seam or body hole.
